content: Turn-based combat animation in browser games benefits from a finite state machine (FSM) pattern. The animation system has distinct states: IDLE, ATTACKING, PROJECTILE_FLIGHT, IMPACT, HEALING, BLOCKING, DYING, DEAD. Transitions are triggered by state diff events from combat resolution. The FSM ensures animations don't overlap or glitch — each hero/monster has its own FSM instance. State machines can be simple (string-based switch) or use a proper FSM library. For vanilla JS, a simple state enum + transition map + onEnter/onExit callbacks is sufficient. Critical: each entity's FSM must be independent — heroes and monsters don't share animation state. Attack chains naturally become sequences of states: IDLE → WINDUP → ATTACK → RECOVERY → IDLE.
